description: Elephant vocalization denoising system for HackSMU - exploits strict harmonic structure of elephant rumbles to surgically extract calls from overlapping mechanical noise (generators, cars, planes). Python + FastAPI + React stack.

userStories.title: Project scaffolding and dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project structure and all dependencies installed so the team can start building in parallel.
acceptanceCriteria: Python project with pyproject.toml or requirements.txt containing: librosa, noisereduce, scipy, numpy, pandas, matplotlib, fastapi, uvicorn, python-multipart
React app scaffolded in frontend/ directory with Vite + TypeScript
Frontend dependencies: react, recharts or wavesurfer.js for spectrogram visualization
Directory structure: pipeline/, api/, frontend/, data/, output/
README.md with setup instructions for all team members
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Spreadsheet parser and call segmenter
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want the system to parse the call timestamp spreadsheet and auto-segment each recording into individual call clips so I can process them independently.
acceptanceCriteria: Reads spreadsheet (CSV/Excel) with columns for filename, start time, end time, call type
Loads each audio file using librosa with sr=None to preserve original sample rate
Extracts individual call segments based on timestamps with configurable padding (default 2s before/after)
Extracts noise-only segments from gaps between calls for noise profiling
Outputs segment metadata as JSON: {file, start, end, call_type, duration, sample_rate}
Handles all 44 recordings and 212 calls without crashing
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the data ingestion foundation. All downstream stages depend on this.

userStories.title: Noise type classifier
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I need to classify noise type (generator, car, plane, mixed) from non-call segments so the denoising strategy can adapt per recording.
acceptanceCriteria: Computes spectral flatness from noise-only segments to distinguish broadband vs tonal noise
Classifies noise into categories: generator (near-constant tonal), car (transient broadband), plane (slow-sweep broadband), mixed/unknown
Generator detection: identifies near-constant spectral signature with fundamental ~30Hz and harmonics
Returns noise profile dict: {type, spectral_flatness, estimated_fundamentals[], power_spectrum}
Works on noise segments extracted by US-002
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Engine noise fundamental ~30Hz with 2nd harmonic at 60Hz directly overlaps elephant rumble fundamentals. Classification drives strategy selection.

userStories.title: Large-window STFT and spectrogram computation
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I need high-resolution spectrograms with n_fft=8192+ so elephant fundamentals at 10-20Hz are actually resolvable.
acceptanceCriteria: Computes STFT with n_fft=8192 (or 16384 for extra resolution) using librosa
Frequency resolution sufficient to distinguish 10Hz bins (verify: sr/n_fft <= 10)
Returns magnitude spectrogram and phase for later reconstruction
Generates and saves visualization-ready spectrogram images (matplotlib) for before/after comparison
Handles variable sample rates from different recordings
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Most teams will use default n_fft=1024 and get garbage resolution below 50Hz. This is a key differentiator.

userStories.title: Harmonic-percussive source separation (HPSS)
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I need to separate harmonic content (elephant rumbles) from percussive/transient content (car sounds, clicks) using HPSS.
acceptanceCriteria: Applies librosa.decompose.hpss() with tuned kernel_size for infrasonic content
Uses median filtering along time axis to enhance horizontal harmonic contours (Zeppelzauer method)
Returns harmonic component spectrogram with enhanced horizontal structures
Percussive component captures transient noise for removal
Before/after spectrogram comparison saved as PNG
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Elephant rumbles are strongly harmonic. HPSS separates them from transient noise. Median filter along time axis is the Zeppelzauer spectro-temporal enhancement.

userStories.title: Subharmonic summation for f0 detection
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I need to detect the fundamental frequency of elephant calls by summing energy at harmonic multiples, since the 2nd harmonic is stronger than the fundamental.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements subharmonic summation: sweeps f0 candidates from 8-25Hz
For each candidate f0, sums power at 2*f0, 3*f0, 4*f0... up to 1000Hz
Detects f0 by working backward from stronger higher harmonics (2nd harmonic is strongest)
Returns detected f0 per time frame with confidence score
Handles cases where fundamental is completely masked by noise
Normalized summation (NSSH method): normalizes by number of harmonics summed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Critical domain insight: 2nd harmonic of elephant rumbles is significantly stronger than fundamental. Detect f0 by looking at stronger harmonics first. NSSH achieves 30% more precision/recall than spectrogram cross-correlation at low SNR.

userStories.title: Harmonic comb mask builder
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I need to build a binary frequency mask that preserves energy only at integer multiples of the detected f0, surgically isolating elephant harmonics.
acceptanceCriteria: Given detected f0, creates narrow bandpass filters at f0, 2*f0, 3*f0... up to 1000Hz
Bandwidth of each band is configurable (default: +/- 5Hz around each harmonic)
Mask is time-varying: follows f0 track across frames as call frequency modulates
Applies mask to magnitude spectrogram, preserving elephant energy and suppressing everything else
Reconstructs audio from masked spectrogram using original phase + istft
Output audio clearly contains elephant call with dramatically reduced noise
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is THE differentiator. The harmonic comb exploits the mathematical structure (integer-multiple harmonics) that is unique to elephant vocalizations.

userStories.title: Residual noise cleanup with spectral gating
userStories.description: As a pipeline developer, I need to apply noisereduce spectral gating on the comb-masked output to clean up remaining artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Applies noisereduce non-stationary spectral gating on comb-masked audio for general cleanup
For generator recordings: uses stationary variant with noise profile from non-call segments
Noise profile sourced from segments identified by US-002
Strategy selection driven by noise type from US-003
Output is final cleaned audio file (WAV format)
SNR improvement measurable compared to input
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: noisereduce non-stationary mode was literally tested on airplane noise overlapping bird calls. Perfect for this use case.

userStories.title: Batch processing pipeline with confidence scoring
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want all 212 calls batch-processed with per-call confidence scores so I can prioritize which cleaned recordings to trust.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrates full pipeline (US-002 through US-008) for all 212 calls
Per-call confidence score (0-100%) based on: harmonics survived count, SNR improvement dB, harmonic integrity (integer ratio check)
Exports each cleaned call as standalone WAV clip named by original file + call index
Generates summary CSV: filename, call_index, original_SNR, cleaned_SNR, confidence, harmonics_detected, f0_estimate
Exports in Raven Pro compatible format (WAV + selection table .txt)
Total processing completes in reasonable time (< 30 min for all 212 calls)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Immediately deployable deliverable. Raven Pro is the standard bioacoustics annotation tool - one-line metadata addition signals we understand their workflow.

userStories.title: FastAPI backend with processing endpoints
userStories.description: As a web user, I need API endpoints to upload recordings, trigger processing, and retrieve results.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/upload - accepts audio file upload, stores in data/ directory
POST /api/process - triggers denoising pipeline on uploaded file, returns job ID
GET /api/status/{job_id} - returns processing status and progress
GET /api/result/{job_id} - returns cleaned audio file + spectrogram data as JSON
GET /api/batch/summary - returns batch processing summary CSV data
CORS enabled for React frontend
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: React spectrogram visualization component
userStories.description: As a judge viewing the demo, I want to see animated before/after spectrograms with the harmonic comb mask overlaid so I can visually understand what the algorithm does.
acceptanceCriteria: Displays noisy input spectrogram on top, clean output spectrogram on bottom
Harmonic comb mask overlaid in distinct color (e.g., cyan) showing what the algorithm kept vs removed
Spectrograms render from JSON data returned by API
Responsive layout that works on a projector/large screen for demo
Loading state while processing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Visual wow factor for judges. Before/after with the comb mask overlay is the money shot.

userStories.title: Audio playback with before/after toggle
userStories.description: As a judge, I want to hear the before and after audio so I can confirm the denoising actually works.
acceptanceCriteria: Play/pause controls for original noisy audio
Play/pause controls for cleaned audio
A/B toggle button to switch between noisy and clean while playing at same timestamp
Waveform visualization synced with playback position
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: LALAL.AI baseline comparison panel
userStories.description: As a presenter, I want to show side-by-side comparison of our results vs generic AI tools (LALAL.AI) to demonstrate why domain-specific approaches win.
acceptanceCriteria: Panel showing: Original | LALAL.AI result | Our result side by side
Spectrograms for all three versions
SNR metrics displayed for each
Narrative text explaining where generic tools fail on infrasonic content
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Framing: 'we tested existing AI tools, they fail on infrasonic bioacoustics, so we built something domain-specific'. Pre-process a few samples through LALAL.AI manually.

userStories.title: Multi-elephant speaker separation
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want overlapping elephant calls separated into individual caller tracks when multiple elephants vocalize simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: Detects multiple f0 tracks when more than one elephant is calling
Identifies crossing harmonics (harmonics from different calls cross; same-caller harmonics never cross)
Assigns separate f0 tracks to each caller using harmonic spacing constraint
Outputs separate cleaned WAV per detected caller
Handles at least 2 simultaneous callers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: STRETCH GOAL. Addresses one of ElephantVoices' hardest unsolved problems. Maps to their questions about elephant 'conversation'.

userStories.title: Confidence dashboard and batch results viewer
userStories.description: As a researcher, I want a dashboard showing all 212 processed calls with confidence scores, sortable and filterable, so I can quickly find the best results.
acceptanceCriteria: Table view of all processed calls with columns: filename, call#, f0, SNR_before, SNR_after, confidence, noise_type
Sortable by any column
Filterable by confidence threshold and noise type
Click any row to view its spectrogram and play audio
Export filtered results as CSV
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
